old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-ou0d","title":"link_layer.rs: Unbounded peer/replay/pinned tables enable memory exhaustion","description":"File: rust/lichen-link/src/link_layer.rs
Lines: 216-218, 292-296

The ReplayProtector and LinkLayer use unbounded HashMaps:

    pub struct ReplayProtector {
        peers: HashMap<PublicKey, PeerReplayState>,  // Line 218
    }

    pub struct LinkLayer {
        peers: HashMap<[u8; 8], PeerIdentity>,       // Line 293
        replay: ReplayProtector,                      // Line 294
        pinned: HashMap<[u8; 8], PublicKey>,         // Line 295
    }

These tables grow without bound as peers are added. In a mesh network, an attacker could:
1. Send frames from many different public keys
2. Each triggers a new entry in ReplayProtector.peers
3. Memory exhaustion when table exceeds available RAM

This is the same class of issue as project-LICHEN-ozob (Python ReplayProtector) but in the Rust implementation.

Fix options:
1. Add a maximum peer count with LRU eviction
2. Rate-limit new peer additions
3. Document the limitation and require application-level peer management

Note: The current design relies on add_peer() being called explicitly, so untrusted frames don't auto-enroll. However, the replay protector is keyed by pubkey which grows on successful verify.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:11:42.64686Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T17:11:42.6469Z"}

However, the replay protector is keyed by pubkey which grows on successful verify.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:11:42.64686Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T22:47:46.25813845Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T22:47:46.25813845Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T22:52:46.25813845Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T22:47:46.25813845Z"}
new_value: {"notes":"Commit: d10de5abd - bounded ReplayProtector, LinkLayer peers and pinned with LRU eviction mirroring announce.rs pattern"}

Implemented LRU eviction with max=32 for ReplayProtector.peers, LinkLayer.peers and pinned tables using access_counter and min_by_key eviction mirroring the pattern in announce.rs; updated receive_frame, peer_auth_state, add/remove_peer, tests; verified no API breakage for public methods
